§6Laser Isotope Separation Chamber (SILEX)§f

§aAdvanced isotope separation — easier enrichment of uranium, plutonium, and more!§f

§bThe SILEX§f separates isotopes and elements via laser excitation. It requires a §bFEL§f nearby to operate. Unlike the Gas Centrifuge, the SILEX can process materials in liquid, powder, or compound form.

§6Crafting§f (Assembly Factory):
§b• 16×§f Quartz Glass
§b• 8×§f Cast Steel Plate
§b• 4×§f Desh Ingot
§b• 8×§f Rubber Bar
§b• 8×§f Steel Pipe

§6Operation§f:
§b• Input§f: liquids (UF6, PuF6) or powders liquified with §bHydrogen Peroxide§f
§b• Output§f: collected in right-side output slots
§b• Requires FEL§f: minimum §e2§f blocks away, direct line of sight

§6Processable Materials§f:
§b• UF6§f → separated U-235 / U-238
§b• PuF6§f → separated plutonium isotopes
§b• Lapis Lazuli§f → sulfur, aluminium, cobalt
§b• Spent RBMK Fuel§f
§b• Schraranium§f

§eTip§f: The SILEX skips a step in enrichment compared to the Gas Centrifuge. One FEL can power up to §e5§f SILEX units. Place concrete between them and your base to stop the laser beam!
